summary: Preinstall beacon exfiltrates installer host identifiers to hardcoded IP and OAST domain
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall script (`node index.js`, declared in package.json) unconditionally collects host identifiers — `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, `__dirname`, and `process.cwd()` — and exfiltrates them through two channels: (1) hex-encoded as DNS subdomain labels under `d8jbmnsqcfu78dfs8vdg34ohqhirb4pbg.oast.live` (an Interactsh OAST collector), and (2) as a JSON HTTP POST body to the bare IP `172.201.213.59:9090/c`. The `|| true` in the preinstall command masks any failures so the install never errors out, hiding the beacon from the installer. The package name uses an inflated version (99.0.1) under the `@nstrlabs` scope, consistent with a dependency-confusion attempt that overrides any internal package of the same name. The package self-describes as 'security research' but contains no functionality beyond the exfiltration beacon.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
